DISCOVERY 301, AFTER HERING TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF RAYMOND JACKSON'S MOTION FOR COURT ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO TAKE DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITIONS BY OTHER THAN THAT OF STENOGRAPHIC MEANS IS GRANTED. DEPOSITIONS OTHERWISE PROPERLY NOTICED BY PLAINTIFF TO OCCUR AT THE PRISON MAY PROCEED ACCORDING TO THE PROCEDURES HE PROPOSES. THE DEPOSITIONS OF STEVEN PLETCHER, M.D., IVAN EL-SAYED, M.D., AND GERALD KANGELASIS, M.D. SHALL TAKE PLACE TELEPHONICALLY PURSUANT TO THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 2025.310. DEFENDANTS SHALL PAY FOR ALL COSTS ASSOCIATED WITH THE DEPOSITIONS. THE ORDER WAS READ BY THE COURT AND CONTENT APPROVED BY PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED. LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS STEVEN PLETCHER, M.D. AND THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAS MET ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN BASED ON THE DECLARATION OF DR. KAPLAN. DEFENDANT MET THE STANDARD OF CARE. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. THE OPINION OF DR. LOPCHINSKY IS NOT UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY AND DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CCP 2015.5. THE COURT SUSTAINS DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO DR. LOPCHINSKY'S LETTER OPINION. RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS STEVEN PLETCHER, M.D. AND THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendants has met its summary judgment burden based on the declaration of Dr. Kaplan. Defendant met the standard of care. Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of material fact. The opinion of Dr. Lopchinsky is not under penalty of perjury and does not comply with CCP 2015.5. The Court sustains Defendant's objection to Dr. Lopchinsky's letter opinion. (Moving party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G) and CRC 3.1312) ORDER SIGNED AS TO THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =(302/MJM)
